preamble: The shift from [o] to [ε] is a variant of the i-umlaut process where a back vowel is fronted and lowered, often in short-vowel contexts.

examples.note: The short 'o' fronts to an open-mid front rounded vowel [œ], which often alternates with [ε] in dialects.

sources: Waterman: A History of the German Language
